title: T-017 — LP split service
prompt:
# T-017 — LP split service

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §6.4 (FR-WH-002), V-WH-LP-003.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Implement `splitLp(sourceLpId, splitQty, destLocationId?)` Server Action that creates a child LP, decrements source, prorates catch_weight, and inserts genealogy row.

## Implementation contract
File `apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/lp-split.ts`. Steps per §6.4 algorithm:
1) validate (status in available/reserved, 0 < splitQty < source.qty)
2) create child LP via T-016 inheriting product/uom/batch/expiry/qa_status/manufacture_date/shelf_life_mode_snapshot, prorated catch_weight
3) UPDATE source.quantity -= splitQty
4) INSERT lp_genealogy (operation_type='split')
5) Optional stock_move to destLocationId
All within a single transaction.

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/lp-split.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/lp-split.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given source.qty=100 and splitQty=40, when service runs, then source becomes qty=60 and child has qty=40 with the same batch.
2. Given splitQty=0 or splitQty>=source.qty, when service runs, then it returns a validation error and rolls back.
3. Given service completes, when lp_genealogy is queried, then a row links source as parent and child with operation_type='split' and quantity=splitQty.

## Test strategy
- RED: lp-split.test.ts covers happy path, invalid qty, and genealogy row.
- Run `pnpm --filter web test lp-split`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not perform any step outside one DB transaction.
- Do not orphan child LP without genealogy.
- Do not allow split on status='consumed'/'shipped'/'blocked'.

pipeline_inputs.description: Implement LP split Server Action that creates child, decrements source, prorates catch_weight, and writes genealogy.
pipeline_inputs.details: Single-transaction algorithm per §6.4. Optional stock_move to destination. Catch-weight proration. Status validation per V-WH-LP-003.

Given splitQty=0 or splitQty >= source.qty, when service runs, then it returns a validation error and rolls back
